data.title: Explained: What is the corporate bond market's new liquidity window facility and who stands to benefit?
data.description:
The issuer can decide which investor can avail the facility. It can be given to all investors or only to retail investors, said the Sebi circular.

The market regulator has launched yet another reform to encourage retail participation in the corporate bond market.

On October 16, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) issued the framework for corporate debt issuers to provide a liquidity window facility to investors in the secondary market.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

This is the latest in a string of steps the regulator has taken in the recent past to improve retail participation. Earlier, it had reduced the minimum face value of bonds to Rs 10,000 from a steep Rs 1 lakh. It had also allowed the registration of online bond platforms.

Here’s a look at what this latest development means for investors and entities who want to raise debt.

Also read: Sebi introduces Liquidity Window to improve participation in corporate-bond market

First, what was the earlier mechanism?

In India, a large part of debt issuances occurs through the private placement mode. These issuances are primarily bought by qualified institutional buyers (QIBs) or non-QIB entities such as corporates, who then sell them forward in the secondary market.

Under the regular system, a typical primary debt issuance could include a put and call option, if required, through the term sheet, allowing both the issuer and the investor to exit on specific dates mentioned in the disclosure document.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

A put option provides the investor with an exit before the bond’s maturity, while a call option allows the issuer to redeem the bond (pay off the principal and close the debt) before maturity.

According to Venkatakrishnan Srinivasan, bond-market veteran and founder and managing partner of Rockfort Fincap LLP, issuances usually come with put-and-call options only when both the investor and the issuer require them.

However, this system does not improve liquidity in the secondary market.

What is this new mechanism?

Under the new facility, which is optional, SEBI is introducing a liquidity window for investors (particularly retail investors) in the generally illiquid secondary market.

In this new mechanism, the issuing company acts as a temporary buyer (only in the secondary market) and can offer a schedule to purchase these bonds, enabling retail investors to exit when they need funds.

The issuer must specify in the disclosure document that a particular issue will offer a liquidity window facility in the secondary market. This is in addition to, or separate from, what is offered in the term sheet to buyers in the primary market.

Srinivasan points out that this is not strictly a “put option” since the debt isn’t immediately redeemed (paid back). Instead, the bonds can be resold to another investor through stock exchanges, RFQ platforms, or online bond portals. Only if the issuer fails to resell the bonds within the stipulated time might they consider extinguishing the bonds.

RFQ platforms or Request for Quote platforms are for investors to directly execute trades in the debt market.

Why was it introduced?

This was introduced to encourage more retail participation in the corporate bond market, according to insiders. This facility should reassure them that there can be sufficient liquidity for a debt issue. Even the SEBI circular said that this was being done to "address the issue of liquidity for investors, especially retail investors".

Can all issuers provide this facility?

Yes, it is left to their discretion.

Can all investors avail the facility?

The issuer can decide which investor class can avail the facility. It can be given to all investors or only to retail investors, said the SEBI circular.

Who stands to benefit the most from this?

According to Srinivasan, apart from retail investors, lower-rated issuers—those with, say, ratings of BBB or lower—in the BFSI sector would benefit the most.

Several Reserve Bank of India directives have indicated that the regulator wants more caution exercised in the issue of unsecured loans by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs), leading to a drying of funds flowing as loans from banks to  NBFCs. Srinivasan believes that these NBFCs could now come into the debt market and raise money from the public at large.

Srinivasan pointed to the discounted rate at which these "buybacks" can be done by the issuer and the operational ease with which these can then be sold forward as particularly helpful to entities going in for smaller issues. "Issuers are allowed to repurchase their securities at up to a 100-basis point discount from the valuation plus accrued interest—remember withdrawing bank FDs (fixed deposits) before maturity?—provided they comply with SEBI’s norms. The flexibility in buybacks, coupled with the option to resell bonds through stock exchanges, RFQ platforms, or online portals—or extinguish them in the worst case—gives issuers efficient ways to manage their debt while supporting investor liquidity."

Vishal Goenka, co-founder of IndiaBonds.com, too believes that this will benefit retail investors in particular. He said, "The circular states that it is up to the issuer to provide the liquidity window to different investors whether retail or institutional. However, we think that this is intended more for retail investors and will benefit them the most."

From the issuer's perspective, smaller-sized entities stand to gain substantially, according to Goenka.

He said, "By the nature of markets, smaller companies are less recognised by investors and, hence, these measures will benefit them the most."

Also read: Sebi moots demat accounts for Association of Persons to hold mutual fund units, corporate bonds, GSec

Why has liquidity been a problem in the bond market?

Goenka said that liquidity or rather its lack in the corporate bond market is an issue faced globally and not just in India. However, there are problems with liquidity that are particular to the Indian market, and Goenka listed them as below:

a) Most of the buyers are hold-to-maturity institutional investors like insurance companies and pension funds. They invest for asset-liability matching and seldom sell bonds before they mature.

b) Companies' requirement for debt capital is still largely fulfilled by banks, which may change soon given the credit/deposit ratio resulting in higher funding costs for lenders

c) Corporate bonds themselves are traded on various entities—RFQ, OTC and capital markets segment of exchanges. There needs to be alignment here to ensure liquidity is concentrated in one place like equities
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Lakshya Powertech SME IPO subscribed 125x on day 2, grey market premium remains robust
data.description:
Lakshya Powertech IPO

The initial public offering of Lakshya Powertech, the engineering consultancy and contracting company, continued to attract strong interest from investors even on the second day of bidding, October 17. The participants bought 25.44 crore equity shares, increasing by 125.18 times over the offer size of 20.28 lakh shares.

The Telangana-based company intends to mobilise Rs 49.91 crore through its maiden public issue at the upper end of price band of Rs 171-180 per share. The SME IPO comprises solely fresh issue by the firm, thus the entire issue proceeds will be received by the company.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The maximum support to the issue to get robust subscription numbers has been received from retail investors and non-institutional investors who have bought 213.9 times and 152.7 times their allotted quotas, respectively, while the portion set aside for qualified institutional buyers was subscribed 9 times.

The public issue has received more than 2.56 lakh applications from investors so far on Thursday.

Lakshya Powertech that specialises in engineering, procurement, construction, and commissioning (EPCC) services has already raised Rs 13.39 crore via anchor book on October 15. Total 11 anchor investors including HDFC Bank, Veloce AIF, Paradise Moon Investment Fund, Coeus Global Opportunities Fund, Vikasa India, Saint Capital Fund, and Abundantia Capital made investment in the company.

The company is going to spend Rs 30 crore for its working capital requirements, Rs 4.5 crore for repaying debt, and the remaining funds for general corporate purposes.

Lakshya Powertech caters to oil and gas, power, industrial, and renewable energy sectors as well as provides operation and maintenance services, has executed more than 138 projects with major completed projects amounting to Rs 268.43 crore. It has 45 major ongoing projects worth Rs 431.99 crore as of August 2024.

Meanwhile, its IPO shares were available at 90-95 percent premium over the upper price band in the grey market, the market observers said. The grey market is an unofficial market wherein IPO shares can be bought and sold till the listing.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

data.title: Technical View: Nifty breaks Bearish Flag, more downside likely if it fails to defend 24,700
data.description:
Nifty Under Pressure

The Nifty 50 fell more than 200 points to reach a nearly two-month low and has decisively breached the Bearish Flag pattern on the daily charts on October 17 (the weekly F&O expiry session), signaling more downside in the upcoming sessions. The index also fell below the rising support trendline, which is another negative sign. The next immediate support lies at 24,700. If the index decisively breaks this level, 24,550 (20-week EMA - Exponential Moving Average) is likely to be a crucial support level to watch. However, on the upside, 25,000, which coincides with the 50-day EMA, is expected to be a hurdle for the index, according to experts.

The Nifty 50 opened higher at 25,027 but immediately moved downward and extended selling pressure as the day progressed. The index hit a day's low of 24,729 before closing at 24,750 (the lowest closing level since August 20), down 221 points or 0.9 percent, and forming a long bearish candlestick pattern on the daily timeframe. The momentum indicators RSI (Relative Strength Index - 38 level) and MACD (Moving Average Convergence Divergence) also indicate a negative trend.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"The Nifty has found initial support in the 24,700–24,750 zone. The RSI is showing a bearish crossover and is declining," said Rupak De, Senior Technical Analyst at LKP Securities.

However, according to him, this may not be the ideal level to initiate short positions, as the index has experienced a steep correction and is near a double-bottom support, which could trigger a near-term recovery toward 25,000.

Conversely, a decisive fall below 24,700 could lead to a significant correction in the market, he noted.

On the weekly options front, the maximum Call open interest was observed at the 24,800 strike, followed by the 25,000 and 25,500 strikes, with maximum Call writing at the 24,800 strike, and then the 24,900 and 25,000 strikes. On the Put side, the 24,700 strike holds the maximum open interest, followed by the 24,500 and 24,800 strikes, with maximum writing at the 24,700 strike, and then the 24,600 and 24,400 strikes.

The above options data also suggested that the Nifty may face resistance at 24,800, followed by 25,000, while the support lies at 24,700 and then 24,500.

Bank Nifty

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Bank Nifty almost wiped out its gains from the start of the week, falling decisively below 51,500. The index was down by 512 points, or 1 percent, at 51,289, and formed a long bearish candlestick pattern on the daily timeframe, breaking the 10 and 50-day EMAs.

The index negated the formation of higher highs and lows from the last three sessions. "The index again got stuck in a wider range, and now, until it holds below the 51,650 zone, some weakness could be seen toward the 51,000 and then 50,500 levels, while on the upside, resistance is seen at the 51,500 and then 51,750 zones," said Chandan Taparia, Senior Vice President | Analyst-Derivatives at Motilal Oswal Financial Services.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: IGL says lower gas allocation will adversely impact profitability
data.description:
Shares of major city gas suppliers IGL, MGL and Gujarat Gas were under selling pressure in trade on October 17

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Indraprastha Gas had confirmed a reduction of 21% in the domestic gas allocation to the company, a factoring that has weighed on the shares all city gas distributor companies on October 17, and said that the decision will hurt profitability.

IGL cited a communication from GAIL India, which is the nodal agency for domestic gas allocation, in its stock exchange filing confirming the development.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Shares of major city gas suppliers IGL, MGL and Gujarat Gas were all under selling pressure in trade, after reports of a large cut in gas allocation through the Administered Price Mechanism (APM) has triggered concerns.

"The revised domestic gas allocation to Company is approximately 21% lesser than previous allocation, which will have an adverse impact on profitability of the Company," IGL's statement to the stock exchanges said.

It is not clear if IGL will undertake a price hike to mitigate the financial burden of sourcing costlier gas. "The Company is in discussion with key stakeholders to minimize the impact."

IGL said it has been receiving CNG at a price of $6.5 per mmbtu, fixed by the Centre, while the spot price of LNG in open market is over $11 per unit.

This is being updated.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Taking Stock: Sensex down 495 pts, Nifty closes below 24,750, dragged by auto, realty, bank stocks
data.description:
Market Today

Market extended the losses on the third consecutive session on October 17, with benchmark Nifty 50 closing a little below 24,750 amid selling across sectors barring Information Technology.

At close, the Sensex was down 494.75 points or 0.61 percent at 81,006.61, and the Nifty was down 221.50 points or 0.89 percent at 24,749.80.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Amid positive global cues, the Indian indices opened higher with Nifty above 25,000 but erased the gains in the initial hours to turned into red and extended the losses as the day progress, dragging Nifty below 24,750, intraday.

Biggest Nifty losers were Bajaj Auto, Shriram Finance, Hero MotoCorp, Nestle and M&M, while gainers were Tech Mahindra, Infosys, L&T, Power Grid Corp and SBI.

Among sectors, except Information Technology index (up 1 percent), all other sectoral indices ended in the red with auto, media and realty down 2-3 percent.

The BSE midcap and smallcap indices shed more than 1 percent each.

More than 240 stocks touched their 52-high on the BSE, including, Strides Pharma, Tech Mahindra, Gujarat Fluoro, CAMS, IFB Industries, Indigo Paints, Firstsource Solutions, CarTrade Tech, ICRA, Dhani Services, Oberoi Realty, among others. Click To View More

Outlook for October 18

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Worst SectorPricesChangeChange%
Nifty Auto24991.20-917.80 -3.54%

Shrikant Chouhan, Head of Equity Research, Kotak Securities:

Today, the benchmark indices witnessed a sharp selloff, the Nifty ends 220 points lower while the Sensex was down by 500 points. Among sectors all the major sectoral indices registered profit booking at higher levels but realty and auto lost the most fell over 3.5 percent. Technically after a muted opening Nifty breached important level of 24900/81500 and post breakdown the selling pressure intensified.

On daily chart the index has formed bearish candle and on intraday charts it is holding a lower top formation. We are of the view that the market texture is weak and as long as Nifty trades below 24900/81500 weak sentiment is likely to continue. On the downside the market could slip till 24550-24500/80900-80600. On the flip side, above 24825/81300 we could see a one quick pullback rally till 24850-24900/81400-81500.

Rupak De, Senior Technical Analyst, LKP Securities

The Nifty has found initial support in the 24,700–24,750 zone. On the daily chart, the Nifty has broken down from a bearish flag pattern, suggesting a possible downward move in the short term.

The RSI is showing a bearish crossover and is declining. However, this may not be the ideal level to initiate short positions, as the index has experienced a steep correction and is near a double-bottom support, which could trigger a near-term recovery toward 25,000. Conversely, a decisive fall below 24,700 could lead to a significant correction in the market.

Aditya Gaggar Director of Progressive Shares

Finally, the Index broke its range of 24,920-25,200. After a muted opening, Auto stocks led correction dragged the Index lower and in the second half of the trading session, another round of selling pulled the Index further lower to end the session at 24,749.85 with a loss of 221.45 points. Barring IT, all the other sectors ended the session in red with Realty and Auto being the major laggards. Broader markets underperformed the Frontline Index as Mid and Smallcaps tanked by 1.66% & 1.24% respectively.

A bearish marubozu open candle on the daily chart depicts a strong domination of bears but the Index stands at a strong support level of 24,750 (tested on 9th September and 7th October).

A break below the mentioned level will drag the Index towards 24,430; and in the case of a reversal, 24,950 will be considered as immediate resistance.

data.title: Wipro Q2 net profit rises 21% to Rs 3,209 cr, IT firm announces bonus shares in proportion of 1:1
data.description:
Wipro Ltd declared Q2 FY25 results today.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

IT firm Wipro Ltd on October 17 announced 21% rise in net profit at Rs 3,209 crore for the September quarter as against Rs 2,646 crore in the year-ago period.

It also announced bonus shares in proportion of 1:1.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

A Moneycontrol poll of brokerages had projected Wipro’s Q2 net profit to marginally increase to Rs 3,011 crore, with revenue expected to grow to Rs 22,219 crore, driven by the ramp-up in new deals.

Ahead of the results announcement, Wipro shares fell 0.65 percent to end at Rs 528.7 on NSE on October 17, while benchmark Nifty 50 fell 0.9 percent for the day.

The company's consolidated revenue fell 1% to Rs 22,302 crore in the July-September quarter from Rs 22,543 crore in the year-ago period.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Indices dip into the red for the third session amid underwhelming earnings and FII selling
data.description:
The Nifty Auto index witnessed a widespread sell-off, with every component tumbling between 0.5-13 percent.

The Sensex and Nifty closed in the red for the third consecutive session on October 17, weighed down by relentless selling from foreign investors, a lacklustre earnings season, and geopolitical tensions. Today's trading was particularly brutal for the banking and auto sectors, with the Nifty Auto index suffering a dramatic plunge of over 3 percent following Bajaj Auto reducing its festive season sales growth guidance to 3-5 percent—well below the anticipated 8 percent.

At close, the Sensex was down 494 points or 0.6 percent at 81,006 and the Nifty was down 221 points or 0.9 percent at 24,749. About 1,199 shares advanced, 2,580 shares declined, and 101 shares remained unchanged.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Analysts expressed their discontent with the earnings reports from heavyweight companies such as Reliance Industries, TCS, and Bajaj Auto. "While the overall optimism about earnings hasn't completely faded, the initial outlook is certainly not encouraging," said Ajit Mishra, SVP of Research at Religare Broking. "For a meaningful market recovery, both IT and banking must maintain their strength."

Follow our live blog for all the market action

The Nifty Auto index witnessed a widespread sell-off, with every component tumbling between 0.5-13 percent. Tyre stocks were also hit, with CEAT, Apollo Tyres, and JK Tyres all dropping over 2 percent.

Nifty Realty, which had shown resilience over the past three sessions, also took a hit today, declining more than 3 percent with Godrej Properties and DLF at the forefront of the losses.

The broader market wasn't spared either, as BSE Midcap and BSE Smallcap indices both fell by 1.5 percent.

Amidst this bloodbath on Nifty's weekly expiry, Nifty IT defied the trend, gaining over 1 percent, driven by Infosys and Tech Mahindra. Investors are keenly awaiting Infosys and Wipro's Q2 FY25 earnings, slated for release after market hours today, and Tech Mahindra's figures due on October 19.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Market participants are also keeping a close eye on key US economic data releases, including September retail sales, industrial production figures, and weekly jobless claims, all set to drop later today.

Disclaimer: Moneycontrol is a part of the Network18 group. Network18 is controlled by Independent Media Trust, of which Reliance Industries is the sole beneficiary.

data.title: Infosys Q2 net profit rises to Rs 6,506 crore, guidance raised further; Rs 21 per share dividend declared
data.description:
Infosys

Infosys Ltd's Q2 FY25 net profit increased to Rs 6,506 crore from Rs 6,368 crore in the previous quarter, missing Street expectations. The Indian IT bellwether's July-September revenue also posted healthy growth, reaching Rs 40,986 crore.

The company raised its full-year revenue growth guidance to 3.75-4.5 percent, topping up on the surprise hike to 3-4 percent announced in July. Analysts from brokerages such as PhillipCapital, Emkay Global, and Nuvama had anticipated the IT major would raise its full-year revenue growth guidance.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

A Moneycontrol poll of 11 brokerages had estimated Infosys' fiscal second-quarter net profit to rise to Rs 6,769 crore, and revenue to increase to Rs 40,857 crore. Growth was projected to be driven by the ramp-up of large deals, increasing traction in generative AI initiatives, and cost optimisations. The company’s acquisition of R&D services provider In-Tech also likely contributed to the growth.

Infosys declared an interim dividend of Rs 21 per share. For FY24, the company had previously declared a final dividend of Rs 20, a special dividend of Rs 8, and an interim dividend of Rs 18.
